title: iLEAP Certification Record
description: An immutable iLEAP certification record issued when a tool passes the iLEAP conformance testing process.

uuid.description: Globally unique identifier for this certification record (UUID v4). Used as the filename.

tool_id.description: UUID of the tool being certified. Must match the 'uuid' field in the corresponding tool record (tools/{tool_id}.json).

certification.description: Details of the iLEAP certification granted.

standard.description: The standard against which the tool was certified.

standard_version.description: Version of the iLEAP Technical Specifications (e.g., '1.0.0').

date.description: ISO 8601 date when certification was granted.

valid_until.description: ISO 8601 date until which this certification is valid (1 year from certification date). Also conditional on the provider's SFC Certification being renewed — see the tool record.

outcome.description: Overall certification outcome. Always 'Passed' — failed attempts are not recorded in this registry.

data_transactions.description: iLEAP Data Transactions and their status in the test run. DT1 and DT2 are mandatory and must be 'tested'. DT3 is optional. DT4 is currently out of scope.

status.description: 'tested': included in the test run and passed. 'not_tested': applicable but not tested (valid for optional DTs only). 'out_of_scope': not applicable to this certification.

test_evidence.description: References to the conformance test runs that evidence this certification.

act_run_id.description: UUID of the iLEAP ACT (Automated Conformance Testing) run, as stored in the ACT database.

act_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the ACT test run was performed.

basepath.description: Base URL of the API endpoint that was tested.

pact_run_id.description: UUID of the PACT Conformance Service test run (from the PACT service response).

exceptions.description: Test cases that did not succeed (Skipped, Failed, or InternalError), each with a rationale explaining why this does not prevent certification. Omitted when all tests passed.

rationale.description: Explanation of why this non-passing result does not prevent certification.
